# The Burrowing Swarm

## Event — Tier 2

*A swarm of silkwing creatures erupts from unstable mining tunnels, bursting through the ground as their habitat collapses beneath industrial greed.*

**Impulses:** Overwhelm intruders, defend the brood, spill into the surface

> **Difficulty:** 15
> **Potential Adversaries:** Silkwings (Silkwing Broodmother, Silkwing Cluster, Silkwing Strider, Silkwing Devourer)

## Environment Features

### Escalating Infestation - Reaction

When the scene begins, start a Consequence Countdown (Loop 20) representing the swarm’s growing pressure beneath the surface.

Each time the PCs roll with Fear, tick down the countdown by 1. As the countdown lowers, the swarm escalates.

- **At 18, 14, 10, 6, and 2:** The ground ruptures as more creatures emerge. Roll 1d4 and spawn that many Silkwing adversaries within Close range of the battlefield.
- **At 16, 12, 8, and 4:** Workers are overwhelmed as the swarm spreads. All Silkwings gain +1 to their damage rolls (cumulative).
- **At 0**: The infestation reaches a critical mass. A *Silkwing Devourer* emerges from beneath the ground. Reset the Consequence Countdown to 20.

*They were always down there. The digging just gave them a way out. How long the mine will hold on?*

### Rally the Workers - Reaction

Not all are lost. Some can still be led to safety. A PC may attempt to rally nearby workers, directing them to safety through collapsing tunnels and unstable ground. The PC may make a Presence Roll. On a success, reduce the swarm's damage bonus by 1 (to a mininum of 0).

*Panic spreads faster than the swarm. Someone has to make them move.*

> **Design notes:** For this combat, I tried something different and tried to explore more Countdowns. I used some creatures from the [Incredible Creatures](https://www.drivethrurpg.com/en/product/544239/incredible-creatures-adversaries-for-daggerheart) by Alan Tucker and my players had a lot of fun with it.
